acceptanceSignals: Plant scope + role guards hold across UI/API.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Employee Portal MVP in 1 attempt

reinforcementResults.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The Employee Portal API changes are synchronized across the atomic interface boundary: backend handlers/routes, OpenAPI artifacts (`contract/openapi/openapi.yaml` and `contract/openapi/openapi.json`), and regenerated TS client artifacts (`contract/generated/ts-client/services/EmployeeService.ts`, `contract/generated/ts-client/models/PickupVerificationQr.ts`, and exports). Local contract checks succeeded (`pnpm run contract:check-client`, `scripts/check-contract-drift.sh`).

reinforcementResults.name: Authorization Boundary Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. Current workspace satisfies the active identity/role/scope policy for Employee Portal MVP: UI guards enforce employee/vendor/admin isolation with scoped path checks (`hooks.server.ts`, `auth/guards.ts`, `apps/web/tests/hooks.test.ts`), token refresh/expiry/rejection paths are covered (`apps/web/tests/runtime.test.ts`), and privileged API role/scope denials are enforced across centralized backend auth boundaries (Bearer JWT validation + role/scope checks in `src/bin/observability_runtime_service.rs`, central authorization in `src/access.rs`/`src/transport/http.rs`, and governance tests in `tests/identity_access_governance.rs` plus runtime role/scope rejection tests). Feature scope evidence is present for week/calendar menus, stock/cutoff-aware ordering, 30-second pickup QR refresh, and payroll ledger/dispute/status workflows.

reinforcementResults.name: Canonical File Storage Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. Employee Portal MVP acceptance signals are implemented across UI and API: week/calendar menu discovery, stock- and cutoff-aware ordering (browse/create/modify/list/pickup), 30-second rotating pickup QR with mobile-oriented rendering, and payroll ledger/dispute submission/status tracking. Plant scope and role guards are enforced in both web middleware and backend handlers. Active object-storage policy is satisfied for current feature scope: upload-plan flow validates MIME and size before persistence targets are issued, no runtime DB base64 artifact-storage path was found, and bucket access is provisioned as private-only (`mc anonymous set none`) in both local and Kubernetes provisioning flows.

reinforcementResults.name: Cache and Event Correctness Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Employee Portal MVP and the active performance/correctness policy are satisfied in the current workspace based on implementation and committed test evidence. Employee browse/order/modify/list/pickup and payroll ledger/dispute/status flows are implemented; plant scope and role guards are enforced across UI and API; and policy checks are covered via cache write-through/invalidation evidence, replay/retry idempotency coverage for consumers, and DLQ routing plus alert-rule wiring after retry thresholds.

reinforcementResults.name: UX Reliability Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. Employee Portal MVP currently satisfies the active policy for this feature: employee browse/order/modify/list/pickup and payroll summary/dispute/status tracking are implemented; plant/role guards are enforced across UI/API; async actions expose loading states and guard against duplicate submits; offline/API failures map to understandable zh-TW copy; and responsive behavior for employee mobile plus vendor/admin desktop-tablet is represented in layout/presentation logic and tests. Test commands could not be executed in this sandbox due EPERM restrictions, so this verdict is based on code and in-repo test evidence.

reinforcementResults.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Employee Portal MVP satisfies current feature responsibility and policy gates in this workspace: employee browse/order/modify/list/pickup flow is implemented; payroll summary + dispute submission/status tracking are implemented; plant-scope and role guards are enforced in both web and API paths; week/calendar menu discovery, stock-aware ordering, cutoff countdown, and 30-second pickup QR refresh are present; core behavior uses contract-backed API methods with server-side bearer auth checks; production runtime persistence is SQL-backed with in-memory persistence constrained to test-only code; optional expansions are explicitly feature-flagged. No blocker that this feature must fix now was found in the current code review pass.

executionConstraints: Implement calendar/week menus, stock-aware ordering, and cutoff countdown.
Render 30-second pickup QR refresh with mobile readability.
Keep in-app notifications core; channel expansion optional.
